content_en:
Tabaqat ash-Shafi'iyyah al-Kubra by as-Subki is not theologically neutral. As-Subki was a committed Ash'ari theologian and a defender of the kalam tradition, and these commitments shaped how he wrote about Shafi'i scholars and about the broader theological history of the school.

As-Subki was particularly concerned with defending the Ash'ari theological tradition against what he regarded as the innovations of Ibn Taymiyyah and the Hanbali Athari school. His Tabaqat reflects this concern: he includes extended theological discussions in the biographies of scholars who were involved in theological controversies, defends Ash'ari positions on the divine attributes, and is critical of scholars he regarded as deviating from the Ash'ari consensus.

This theological orientation means that Tabaqat ash-Shafi'iyyah al-Kubra must be read with awareness of the author's perspective. As-Subki's characterizations of scholars who held Athari or Hanbali positions are sometimes unfair or polemical. Conversely, his treatment of Ash'ari scholars is often laudatory in ways that go beyond what the evidence strictly supports. Scholars consulting the work as a historical source need to distinguish between documented facts and as-Subki's interpretive framework.

At the same time, as-Subki's theological concerns make the work particularly valuable for understanding the relationship between the Shafi'i legal school and the Ash'ari theological school — a relationship that was not accidental. The Shafi'i school had historically been closely associated with the Ash'ari tradition: ash-Shafi'i's student al-Muzani was part of the intellectual environment from which al-Ash'ari emerged, and major Shafi'i scholars of subsequent generations — al-Juwayni, al-Ghazali, ar-Razi — were also prominent Ash'ari theologians. As-Subki's documentation of this connection, however colored by his own commitments, is historically valuable.

The extended theological discussions that as-Subki includes in the Tabaqat — discussions of specific theological controversies, defenses of Ash'ari positions, and analyses of where individual scholars stood on disputed questions — make the work unusually rich as a source for the history of Islamic theology as well as for Islamic legal biography.
content_id:
Thabaqat asy-Syafi'iyyah al-Kubra karya as-Subki tidak netral secara teologis. As-Subki adalah teolog Asy'ari yang berkomitmen dan pembela tradisi kalam, dan komitmen-komitmen ini membentuk cara beliau menulis tentang ulama Syafi'i dan tentang sejarah teologis mazhab yang lebih luas.

As-Subki sangat peduli untuk membela tradisi teologis Asy'ari terhadap apa yang ia anggap sebagai inovasi-inovasi Ibn Taymiyyah dan mazhab Hanbali Athari. Thabaqat-nya mencerminkan kepedulian ini: beliau menyertakan pembahasan-pembahasan teologis panjang dalam biografi-biografi ulama yang terlibat dalam kontroversi-kontroversi teologis, membela posisi-posisi Asy'ari atas sifat-sifat ilahi, dan kritis terhadap ulama-ulama yang ia anggap menyimpang dari konsensus Asy'ari.

Orientasi teologis ini berarti bahwa Thabaqat asy-Syafi'iyyah al-Kubra harus dibaca dengan kesadaran tentang perspektif pengarang. Karakterisasi as-Subki tentang ulama-ulama yang memegang posisi Athari atau Hanbali terkadang tidak adil atau polemis. Sebaliknya, perlakuannya terhadap ulama-ulama Asy'ari seringkali memuji melampaui apa yang bukti secara ketat mendukung. Ulama-ulama yang mengkonsultasikan karya ini sebagai sumber sejarah perlu membedakan antara fakta-fakta yang terdokumentasi dan kerangka interpretatif as-Subki.

Pada saat yang sama, kepedulian teologis as-Subki membuat karya ini sangat berharga untuk memahami hubungan antara mazhab Syafi'i dan mazhab Asy'ari — hubungan yang tidak kebetulan. Mazhab Syafi'i secara historis memiliki hubungan erat dengan tradisi Asy'ari: murid asy-Syafi'i, al-Muzani, merupakan bagian dari lingkungan intelektual dari mana al-Asy'ari muncul, dan ulama-ulama Syafi'i besar generasi berikutnya — al-Juwaini, al-Ghazali, ar-Razi — juga merupakan teolog-teolog Asy'ari terkemuka. Dokumentasi as-Subki tentang hubungan ini, betapapun diwarnai oleh komitmen-komitmennya sendiri, sangat berharga secara historis.

Pembahasan-pembahasan teologis panjang yang as-Subki sertakan dalam Thabaqat — pembahasan-pembahasan tentang kontroversi-kontroversi teologis tertentu, pembelaan posisi-posisi Asy'ari, dan analisis di mana ulama-ulama individual berdiri dalam pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang diperdebatkan — menjadikan karya ini sangat kaya sebagai sumber untuk sejarah teologi Islam serta biografi hukum Islam.
content_ar: Tabaqat ash-Shafi'iyyah al-Kubra by as-Subki is not theologically neutral. As-Subki was a committed الأشعري theologian and a defender of the علم الكلام التراث, and these commitments shaped how كتب about الشافعي العلماء and about the broader theological history of the school. As-Subki was particularly concerned with defending the الأشعري theological التراث against what he regarded as the innovations of ابن تيمية and the الحنبلي الأثري school. His Tabaqat reflects this concern: he includes extended theological discussions in the biographies of العلماء who were involved in theological controversies, defends الأشعري positions on the divine attributes, and is البالغ الأهمية of العلماء he regarded as deviating from the الأشعري consensus. This theological orientation means that Tabaqat ash-Shafi'iyyah al-Kubra must be read with awareness of the author's perspective. As-Subki's characterizations of العلماء who held الأثري or الحنبلي positions are sometimes unfair or polemical. Conversely, his treatment of الأشعري العلماء is often laudatory in ways that go beyond what the evidence strictly supports. العلماء consulting the work as a historical source need to distinguish between documented facts and as-Subki's interpretive framework. At the same time, as-Subki's theological concerns make the work particularly valuable for الفهم the relationship between the الشافعي legal school and the الأشعري theological school — a relationship that was not accidental. The الشافعي school had historically been closely associated with the الأشعري التراث: ash-الشافعي's student al-Muzani was part of the intellectual environment from which al-الأشعري emerged, and major الشافعي العلماء of subsequent generations — al-Juwayni, al-Ghazali, ar-Razi — were أيضاً prominent الأشعري theologians. As-Subki's documentation of this connection, غير أن colored by his own commitments, is historically valuable. The extended theological discussions that as-Subki includes in the Tabaqat — discussions of specific theological controversies, defenses of الأشعري positions, and analyses of where individual العلماء stood on disputed questions — make the work unusually rich as a source for the history of Islamic theology as well أما Islamic legal biography.
